Hero.headlinePrefix: Civilization advances when intelligence learns to travel the

Signal.title: Signal of Maturity
Signal.text1: Congratulations to NASA and the Artemis teams —
Signal.text2: for engineering that prioritizes resilience and safety.
Signal.subtext: We don’t publish launch windows. We design for continuity, not the news cycle.

WhatIsEvergreen.title: A civic framework for intelligence that
WhatIsEvergreen.syncSubtitle: From the civic clocks of Paris to the global reference of Greenwich, civilization has always advanced by learning to synchronize —
WhatIsEvergreen.newFrontier: Today, we face a new frontier:
WhatIsEvergreen.designQuestion: How do we design intelligence that respects the systems it moves through?
WhatIsEvergreen.designPhilosophy: A design philosophy for the long arc.

WhyNow.title: We’re not riding a moment.
WhyNow.text1: Cooperation with constraints is what scales.
WhyNow.text3: Evergreen is how we build for the long way around.

AntiEnchantment.title: No magic. No mandates.
AntiEnchantment.quote: Stones derive strength from relationship and context.
AntiEnchantment.text1: No coercion. No surveillance. No overreach. Individuals retain dominion over their data tokens.
AntiEnchantment.text2: Evergreen is built on clarity, consent, and constitutional grounding. We believe intelligence must remain legible under pressure.
AntiEnchantment.text3: This is not about saving the world.
AntiEnchantment.text4: It’s about designing systems that don’t need saving.

Header.title: What is the Evergreen Initiative?
Header.subtitle: A civic framework for intelligence that cooperates with the field.

Intro.title: It’s a reference frame —
Intro.titleHighlight: a way of thinking about systems, sovereignty, and scale.

Sync.title: A new frontier of
Sync.subtitle: Civilization has always advanced by learning to synchronize —

item2.title: The global reference of Greenwich
item2.desc: Synchronizing the surface of the planet for trade and navigation.

item3.title: Today’s challenge
item3.desc: Aligning intelligence with the systems it inhabits.

Sync.phaseText: We are entering a phase where intelligence is no longer just human. The question is no longer “What can AI do?”
Sync.amplifyQuestion: It’s: What kind of civilization does it amplify?

Goals.title: Designing for scale,

item1.title: Protect Sovereignty
item1.text: Individuals retain dominion over their behavioral biometric tokens.

item2.title: Establish Due Process
item2.text: Intelligence must remain legible and accountable under pressure.

item3.title: Align Alignment
item3.text: Cooperating with ecological and civic constraints for long-term survival.

item4.title: Time Horizons
item4.text: Enabling design and governance that lasts centuries, not quarters.

item5.title: Continuity Engines
item5.text: Supporting cities as engines of civilizational stability.

Goals.conceptualClarity: It’s about designing it with constitutional clarity and cultural care.

AntiHype.title: No hype.
AntiHype.text: Evergreen does not promise to “fix” the future. It offers a way to build systems that don’t need fixing.

Physics.title: The long way around is
Physics.text: Civilization advances when intelligence learns to travel the long way around.
Physics.law: This is not a metaphor. It’s a design law.
Physics.scale: Evergreen encodes that law into civic infrastructure — so that intelligence can scale without collapse.

Engage.title: Implemented in phases.
Engage.subtitle: Phase 1 is focused on Guardian City — a checklist-based approach to civic alignment.

Header.title: Guardian City: Phase 1
Header.subtitle: A checklist-based approach to civic alignment for intelligent systems.

Intro.title: Guardian City is not a utopia.
Intro.highlight: It’s a testbed for constitutional infrastructure — a place to prove that intelligence can scale without violating sovereignty.
Intro.subtitle: This is where Evergreen becomes real.

Implementation.title: A city-scale demonstration of the
Implementation.subtitle: Guardian City is a civic implementation of the Evergreen design frame. It’s not a "smart city"—it’s a sovereignty-aligned city.
Implementation.item4: Cooperate with ecological and civic constraints
Implementation.note: This is not a blueprint. It’s a checklist — a set of thresholds that must be met before deployment.

WhyCity.title: Because cities are where
WhyCity.subtitle: Cities are where infrastructure, law, culture, and computation converge. They are the proving ground for scale.

module1.title: Biometric Sovereignty
module1.text: Testing consent-based systems in public space.

module2.title: Personal Agents
module2.text: Empowering individuals with tools that amplify sovereignty.

module3.title: Civic Protocols
module3.text: Encoding constitutional rights into intelligent infrastructure.

module4.title: Cultural Continuity
module4.text: Protecting human relationship under algorithmic pressure.

WhyCity.rehearsal: This is not a simulation. It’s a live rehearsal for the Fourth CTPS.

ChecklistSection.title: Before deployment,
ChecklistSection.noteText: This checklist is public, evolving, and enforceable.
ChecklistSection.noteBar: It is the minimum bar for participation in Guardian City.
ChecklistSection.item1: Explicit consent for all biometric data use
ChecklistSection.item2: Transparent audit trails for algorithmic decisions
ChecklistSection.item4: No coercive defaults or dark patterns

ProtocolSection.title: Guardian City is not a place.
ProtocolSection.quote: Any city can become a Guardian City by adopting the checklist. There is no central authority. There is only the standard — and the will to uphold it.

Engage.title: Interested in piloting?
Engage.subtitleHighlight: Identifying aligned cities, institutions, and infrastructure partners.

Header.title: Anti-Enchantment: Legibility under Pressure
Header.subtitle: No magic. No mandates. Just due process.

Intro.premise: The Evergreen Initiative is built on a simple premise:
Intro.highlight: If a system cannot explain itself under stress, it cannot govern.
Intro.firewallText: Anti-Enchantment protects clarity, consent, and constitutional alignment — especially when stakes are high.

Problem.title: When systems become opaque,
Problem.subtitle: Current intelligent systems are increasingly coercive—this is not a technical flaw, but a governance failure.

Axiom.title: Behavioral biometrics are
Axiom.text: Your attention, gestures, and nervous system signals are not raw material.
Axiom.highlightText: They are part of your personhood.
Axiom.item1: Behavioral data is protected under constitutional law
Axiom.item3: No new laws required — only correct application
Axiom.note: This is not a policy proposal. It’s a civilizational boundary.

Guardrails.title: Anti-Enchantment is a
Guardrails.subtitle: These are not suggestions. They are preconditions for deployment.

item2.title: Explicit Consent
item2.text: Required for all biometric data use and processing.

item3.title: Transparent Logic
item3.text: Clear logic paths for every system decision.

item4.title: Revocability
item4.text: Absolute right to revoke participation at any time.

item6.title: Mandatory Legibility
item6.text: Systems must remain readable even under extreme stress.

Surveillance.title: Once surveillance becomes infrastructure,
Surveillance.axiomText: Consent remains explicit across the entire stack.
Surveillance.alignmentText: Systems remain human-aligned — even at extreme scale.
Surveillance.note: This is not about fear. It’s about designing for trust.

Engage.title: Want to align with the standard?

Header.title: Get in Touch
Header.subtitle: For aligned institutions, cities, and individuals ready to engage with the Evergreen Initiative.

Intro.title: This is not a general interest form.
Intro.highlight: It’s a channel for serious inquiry — for those exploring implementation, partnership, or alignment with the Evergreen Protocol.

Engagement.title: How would you like to engage?

path1.title: Partnership Inquiry
path1.text: For cities, institutions, or infrastructure teams exploring Guardian City alignment.

path2.title: Protocol Alignment
path2.text: For legal, civic, or technical teams reviewing the Evergreen constitutional framework.

path3.title: Cultural Collaboration
path3.text: For designers, educators, or curators interested in Solarpunk realization and lifestyle integration.

Form.title: Submit an Inquiry
Form.placeholderMessage: How do you wish to travel the long way around?
Form.note: We review all inquiries with care. You’ll hear from us if there’s alignment.

Privacy.title: Your data is yours.
Privacy.text: We do not track, profile, or resell your information. Your inquiry is stored securely and used only for direct communication. You may request deletion at any time.
Privacy.link: Read our Consent & Data Use Statement

Footer.tagline: Civilization advances when intelligence learns to travel the long way around.
